seoTitle: Port Macdonnell vs Port Moorowie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Macdonnell and Port Moorowie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Macdonnell vs Port Moorowie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Macdonnell and Port Moorowie.
heading: Port Macdonnell vs Port Moorowie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Macdonnell, South Australia and Port Moorowie, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Macdonnell has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Port Moorowie (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Macdonnell or Port Moorowie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Macdonnell has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Port Moorowie scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Macdonnell scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Port Moorowie scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Macdonnell or Port Moorowie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Macdonnell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
